popularStory.headline: Robert II, Archbishop of Rouen (bef. 989 - 1037)
popularStory.description:
Robert II, Archbishop of Rouen (bef. 989 - 1037), and Count of Évreux was a powerful and influential prelate, and a family member of and supporter of five dukes of Normandy.

He receives the archbisho …

zh.lifeSummary: Robert de Normandie是Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen和Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen的兒子，出生在法國諾門迪艾艾福。他和Herleva of Falaise至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1037年3月16日在他的家鄉，埋葬在法國豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利費艾坎普。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Normandie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Alain, Serge, Adrien, Andre, Aurore, Celine, Clovis, Emile, Gaetan, Gilles, Laurier.
French: from a diminutive of   Normand  . Compare   Normandy  .

variants.name: Normand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gaston, Andre, Laurent, Marcel, Pierre, Amie, Donat, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean Noel, Jean-Michel.
French and Breton (mainly Finistère; also Le Normand): habitational name for someone from Normandy, a region in northern France (see Norman ). Compare Lenormand .

variants.name: Normandy
meanings: English: habitational name from Normandy in northern France. This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Robert de Normandie was born in Évreux, Eure, Normandy, France as the son of Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen and Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen. He had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Herleva of Falaise. He died on 16 March 1037, in his hometown, and was buried in Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Upper Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Robert de Normandie est né à Évreux, Eure, Normandie, France, fils de Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen et Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Herleva of Falaise. Il est décédé le 16 mars 1037, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Robert de Normandie wurde in Évreux, Eure, Normandie, Frankreich, als Sohn von Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen und Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen geboren. Er hatte mit Herleva of Falaise mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 16. März 1037 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Robert de Normandie è nato il nel Évreux, Eure, Normandie, Francia come figlio di Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen e Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Herleva of Falaise. Morì il 16 marzo 1037, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de NormandieはフランスノーマンディエウアevレウックスでRichard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de RouenとGunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouenの息子として生まれた。彼はHerleva of Falaiseとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1037年3月16日に故郷で死去し，フランスハウテ-ノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメフeカンップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Normandie(은)는 프랑스 노먼디 우르 e브레우스에서 Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen(와)과 Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Herleva of Falaise(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1037년 3월 16일 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 프랑스 하테-노먼디 세이인-마이팀 프e캠프에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Robert de Normandie nasceu em Évreux, Eure, Normandie, França filho de Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen e Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Herleva of Falaise. Ele faleceu em 16 de março de 1037, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Robert de Normandie родился в местности eвреукс, эур, норманди, Франция. Его родителей звали Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen и Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Herleva of Falaise. Он умер 16 марта 1037 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности фeкамп, сине-маритим, хауте-норманди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Robert de Normandie nació en Évreux, Eure, Normandie, Francia como hijo de Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie Comte de Rouen y Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Herleva of Falaise. Murió el 16 de marzo de 1037, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia.
